title: Post-cardiology admission follow-up

investigations.text: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.
investigations.rationale: NG185 supports discharge-summary, rehabilitation, symptom-change, question, communication, and follow-up documentation when the cardiology admission involved acute coronary syndrome; it does not generalise that diagnosis to every admission.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.8.34 — discharge information and cardiac rehabilitation

referral.text: Supports regular primary-care review, record updates, medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, follow-up planning, and specialist access.
referral.rationale: Heart-failure care-plan review was checked as a conditional alternative admission context and was not used to infer heart failure.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports regular primary-care review, record updates, medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, follow-up planning, and specialist access.

source.title: Chronic heart failure in adults: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.4–1.1.9 — primary-care review, record, care plan, and access

source_limitations: Admission diagnosis and date, procedures, complications, discharge condition, medicine reconciliation, pending results, symptoms, wound, rehabilitation, restrictions, clinician assessment, plan, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
